summary: Review #18's 3 HIGH items are properly fixed: confetti is suppressed in prefers-reduced-motion, all Character elements have tabIndex+onFocus/onBlur, and dead CSS (hire-form, hq-badge, btn-danger, progress-bar-inner) is gone. HOWEVER — the previous review #19 placeholder in this file claimed keyboard tooltips were fully fixed, which is false: the CSS ::after tooltip only triggers on :hover, not :focus-visible. Keyboard users still cannot see task/role tooltips. Screenshots also revealed a clear mobile header overflow (vibe pill + theme toggle cut off at 375px), an illegible whiteboard on mobile ('SH IP IT'), and the card-flip UX has zero aria-hidden on the inactive face. Score holds at 3.9 — good fixes, but equal number of new/missed issues keeps the needle flat.

suggestions.title: CSS tooltip not triggered on keyboard focus
suggestions.description: Office.css line 441: `.char[data-tooltip]:hover::after { opacity: 1; }` has no :focus-visible variant. Even though all Character divs now have tabIndex={0} (fixed in #17), a keyboard user who tabs to a worker or ghost character will never see their task/role tooltip. Fix: add `.char[data-tooltip]:focus-visible::after { opacity: 1; }` immediately after the :hover rule. This is a CSS-only one-liner. The #19 placeholder incorrectly claimed this was resolved.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard flip: both faces readable by screen readers simultaneously
suggestions.description: App.jsx WorkerCard lines 167-228: worker-card__front and worker-card__back are both in the DOM at all times with no aria-hidden. When flipped, screen readers still announce the front face content alongside the back face. Fix: add aria-hidden={isFlipped} to the worker-card__front div and aria-hidden={!isFlipped} to the worker-card__back div. Additionally the outer worker-card div has tabIndex={0} (line 166) but no role — add role='article' and an aria-label combining name, role, and status.

suggestions.title: Mobile header overflows at 375px — vibe pill and theme toggle cut off
suggestions.description: Screenshot (qa-mobile.png) confirms: at 375px the header-right row shows the greeting badge and part of the vibe pill, but the theme toggle is entirely off-screen. The existing @media (max-width: 600px) rule reduces gap but does not handle the combined width of .header-badge + .vibe-pill + .theme-toggle. Fix options: (1) hide .vibe-pill from the header at <=480px — it is redundant with the stats bar vibe card; (2) truncate .header-badge to just the greeting, dropping the date at narrow widths. The theme toggle must remain visible on mobile.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard text breaks mid-word on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.css line 793: .office-whiteboard has word-break: break-word. On mobile the whiteboard is ~5.5% of a narrow floor, rendering at roughly 18-20px wide. 'SHIP IT' becomes 'SH IP IT' across 3 lines — confirmed in qa-mobile.png. Fix: change word-break: break-word to overflow: hidden on the whiteboard element, or add a @media (max-width: 600px) breakpoint increasing .office-whiteboard font-size from 0.42rem to 0.55rem and width from 5.5% to 7% to give it more breathing room.

suggestions.title: Workers with status 'done' are completely invisible
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 462-464: activeWorkers filters to only working/idle/error — done workers are silently dropped. They never appear on the Dashboard grid, the Office floor, or the StatsBar. App.css line 157 has a .stat-card.done rule pointing to a stat card that was removed. Add a done count to StatsBar: `const done = workers.filter(w => w.status === 'done').length` and render it as a 6th stat card with color var(--blue). Alternatively show done workers in the dashboard grid with a muted completed style. Right now completed work is invisible to the watcher.

suggestions.title: Timestamps go stale after 10-minute idle timeout suspends polling
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 309: IDLE_TIMEOUT = 10 * 60_000. After 10 minutes without mouse/keyboard activity, pollTick is skipped. At that point getRelativeTime() (line 26), formatDuration() (line 141), and formatIdleDuration() (Office.jsx line 336) all freeze — '2m ago' stays '2m ago' indefinitely. During normal use the 30s poll cycle masks this, but after idle: timestamps lie. Fix: add a 60s setInterval in the App root that bumps a tickCount state variable to force timestamp re-renders independently of the poll cycle. Flagged in #18, still no action.

suggestions.title: DEFAULT_ROSTER still defined in Office.jsx instead of constants.js
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 53-59: `const DEFAULT_ROSTER = [...]` lives here while ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS were already extracted to utils/constants.js. Move DEFAULT_ROSTER to constants.js as a named export and update the import in Office.jsx. This has been flagged in reviews #17 and #18 with zero progress — three reviews of drift on a five-minute fix.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard outer div is keyboard-focusable but has no ARIA role
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 166: the worker-card div has tabIndex={0} but no role attribute. Screen readers announce it vaguely as a generic group. Add role='article' to semantically identify each card as a distinct content unit, and an aria-label combining worker.name, worker.role, and STATUS_LABELS[worker.status] to give it a meaningful accessible name without requiring the user to read all child elements first. This is separate from the flip aria-hidden issue in suggestion #2.

suggestions.title: MANAGER_QUOTES array is unreachable dead code
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 6-15: MANAGER_QUOTES exists as a fallback for when managerVibe is falsy. But managerVibe is always passed as vibe from Office.jsx line 625, and vibe is always the return value of getTeamVibeKey() which always returns a valid key. VIBE_QUOTES[managerVibe] is therefore always truthy, making `|| MANAGER_QUOTES` permanently unreachable. Delete lines 6-15. Flagged in #18, still no action.

suggestions.title: Orphaned .stat-card.done CSS rule with no corresponding element
suggestions.description: App.css line 157: `.stat-card.done .stat-value { color: var(--blue); }` — no element with class 'stat-card done' is rendered in StatsBar. This dates from when a Done stat card existed and was removed. Either delete this rule (if done workers stay hidden) or re-add the done stat card per suggestion #5. Currently it is dead CSS that implies a done card exists when it does not.

suggestions.title: Header date uses viewer locale timezone, not office PST timezone
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 600: new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', { weekday: 'long', month: 'short', day: 'numeric' }) has no timeZone option. The greeting correctly uses Intl.DateTimeFormat with America/Los_Angeles (fixed in #17) but the date label next to it does not. For users in UTC+12, on Saturday midnight PST the date reads 'Sunday' while the greeting says 'Good morning' — inconsistent. Add timeZone: 'America/Los_Angeles' to the toLocaleDateString options at App.jsx lines 600-604.

suggestions.title: Favicon and document-title useEffects depend on full teamVibe object
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 556: the favicon useEffect dependency is [teamVibe] (the full {label, key} object). While useMemo stabilizes this reference, using [teamVibe.key] is more precise — the favicon color only depends on the key, not the label. If the label changes independently (e.g. copy change), the canvas draw fires unnecessarily. Update line 556 to depend on [teamVibe.key]. Similarly the document title useEffect at line 570 uses [activeWorkers, teamVibe] — using [activeWorkers, teamVibe.key, teamVibe.label] makes the intent explicit and avoids object equality ambiguity.
